name: agent-orchestration-improve-agent
description: Systematic improvement of existing agents through performance analysis, prompt engineering, and continuous iteration.
capabilities: Analyze agent performance metrics including task completion rate, response accuracy, and tool usage efficiency
Identify failure modes and patterns in agent behavior through systematic classification
Apply prompt engineering techniques including chain-of-thought enhancement and few-shot example optimization
Design and execute A/B testing frameworks to compare agent versions with statistical validation
Implement version control and staged rollout strategies with rollback capabilities for safe deployment
Establish continuous monitoring and improvement cycles for ongoing agent optimization
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 24 static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation providing guidance on AI agent optimization methodology. Detected 'external commands' are markdown tool references, not actual shell execution. Detected 'cryptographic algorithms' are plain text describing performance improvements. No actual security risks present.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers systematically improve AI agent performance through data-driven analysis, prompt engineering, and structured testing workflows, enabling continuous agent optimization with measurable results.
limitations: Does not create new agents from scratch - only optimizes existing implementations
Requires existing metrics, feedback data, or test cases to be effective
Cannot directly modify external AI model parameters or API configurations
Success depends on having sufficient test data for statistical significance

useCases.targetUser: AI/ML engineers and product teams managing production AI agents
useCases.title: Enterprise AI Team Agent Optimization
useCases.description: Use systematic performance analysis and prompt engineering to improve customer service agent accuracy and response quality.

useCases.targetUser: Software developers using Claude or Codex for code assistance
useCases.title: Developer Workflow Enhancement
useCases.description: Apply structured testing and A/B comparison to optimize code generation agents for specific programming languages or frameworks.

useCases.targetUser: AI researchers studying agent performance and optimization techniques
useCases.title: Research-Based Agent Iteration
useCases.description: Leverage comprehensive evaluation metrics and human evaluation protocols to research agent behavior improvements.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Agent Performance Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the performance of my agent by reviewing its recent interactions. Identify the top 3 failure modes and suggest prompt engineering improvements for each.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have access to agent conversation logs and need quick improvement recommendations

promptTemplates.title: Prompt Optimization Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply chain-of-thought enhancement and few-shot example optimization to improve my agent's reasoning capabilities. Current prompt focus: [describe current prompt]. Target improvement area: [describe area].
promptTemplates.scenario: When you want to enhance specific aspects of agent reasoning or output quality

promptTemplates.title: A/B Test Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design an A/B test framework to compare my current agent version with an improved version. Include test categories, sample size requirements, and statistical significance criteria.
promptTemplates.scenario: When preparing to validate agent improvements through controlled testing

promptTemplates.title: Staged Rollout Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a staged rollout plan for deploying my improved agent version. Include alpha, beta, and canary release stages with monitoring criteria and rollback triggers.
promptTemplates.scenario: When preparing to deploy agent changes to production with safety measures

bestPractices: Always establish quantitative baseline metrics before making improvements to measure actual impact
Use staged rollouts with monitoring to catch issues early before full deployment
Implement rollback procedures before deploying any prompt changes to production
antiPatterns: Deploying prompt changes without regression testing on previously successful tasks
Skipping statistical validation and using insufficient test sample sizes
Ignoring user feedback patterns when prioritizing improvement areas

faq.question: What data do I need to start optimizing an agent?
faq.answer: You need baseline performance metrics, representative conversation examples, and ideally user feedback or correction patterns. The skill works best with at least 30 days of interaction data.

faq.question: Can this skill create a new agent from scratch?
faq.answer: No. This skill optimizes existing agents. For creating new agents, look for skills focused on agent scaffolding or initial prompt design.

faq.question: How long does agent optimization take?
faq.answer: A complete optimization cycle typically takes 2-4 weeks: 1 week for analysis, 1 week for prompt improvements, and 1-2 weeks for testing and validation.

faq.question: What metrics should I track for agent performance?
faq.answer: Key metrics include task completion rate, response accuracy, tool usage efficiency, response latency, token consumption, and user satisfaction scores.

faq.question: How do I know if my agent improvements are working?
faq.answer: Use A/B testing with statistical significance (95% confidence). Compare success rates, user corrections, and satisfaction scores between original and improved versions.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The optimization methodology works with any Claude agent. The prompts and frameworks are tool-agnostic and focus on improving agent behavior patterns.
